info.title: Scripture Knowledge Engine
info.description: API for the scripture connection graph — 1,356,667 connections across 124 types in 11 layers, Hebrew + Greek + Vulgate, PaRDeS levels, hidden patterns, lexicon

get.description:
Get targeted remediation drill for a specific failure pattern.

Patterns: can't_start, mid_verse_loss, ending_loss, confusion
Returns exercises and explanations tailored to the failure point.

Process an FSRS-5 review with FIRe, learning speed, and failure tracking.

Rating: 1=Again, 2=Hard, 3=Good, 4=Easy.
hint_level: which hint level was used (0=no hint, 1=first letters, 2=image, etc.)
failure_location: 'start', 'middle', 'end', 'confusion', or '' for no failure.
Learning speed adjusts the interval: faster learners → longer intervals.
If learning_speed < 0.5, FIRe credit is skipped (force explicit reviews).

Get student-topic learning speeds for all practiced nodes.

Returns ability, difficulty per topic, and the ratio (learning speed).
Higher speed = learner is faster on this topic → longer intervals.

Generate a diagnostic pre-assessment covering all categories.

Returns 2-3 sample questions per category to determine what
the learner already knows. Results can skip mastered categories.
Get gamification state: XP, streak, badges, and next achievements.
Discover connections for a node through the scripture graph and earn Insight XP.
Return the AGENTS-style system prompt and tool definitions for the chat LLM.

post.description:
Proxy chat requests to DeepSeek API with function calling support.

If the LLM requests a tool call, the server executes it against the
scripture engine and feeds the result back to the LLM for a final response.

get.description: Get a thematic study guide — ordered progression of verses on a theme.
List all available thematic study guides.
List all study guides.

post.description: Create a new study guide with optional initial steps.

get.description: List all published studies, most recent first.

post.description: Import a study from a JSON string.
Fork a published study into a new mutable study guide.

get.description: Download a published study as JSON. Slug must not have .json extension.
Download a published study as self-contained HTML. Slug must not have .html extension.
Get a published study by its slug.
Get a study guide with enriched steps and graph paths.
Export a study guide as JSON with full graph paths.
Export a study guide as a self-contained HTML page.

post.description: Publish a study as an immutable snapshot with a shareable URL.
Add a step to a study guide.

put.description: Replace all steps of a study guide (deletes existing, inserts new).

delete.description: Remove a step from a study guide and re-number remaining steps.

get.description: List conversation sessions, paginated.

post.description: Create a new conversation session.

get.description: Get a conversation session with all messages, refs, and connections.

patch.description: Update session title or starred status.

post.description: Add a message to a conversation. Auto-extracts verse refs and detects connections.
Add multiple messages at once (for page reload recovery).

get.description: List connections discovered/retrieved in a conversation.

post.description: Manually add a connection to a session.
Promote a conversation connection to the main connection graph.

get.description:
Get deep scripture understanding questions from the assessment_items table.

Questions show passage text, test analysis/understanding, and are tier-labeled.
Replaces the old BLIM-based assessment engine for self-testing.

parameters.name: tier

schema.description: Filter by tier: text, analysis, consistency
schema.title: Tier

parameters.description: Filter by tier: text, analysis, consistency

post.description: Record a quiz answer and update adaptive progress.
Start an adaptive assessment session. Returns first question.
Submit an answer, get next question.

get.description: Get current assessment progress.

post.description:
Sign in with a Google credential token.

Body: { "credential": "...", "anonymous_id": "anon_abc123" }

Verifies the Google token, finds or creates a user, and returns
a session token. If anonymous_id is provided and no existing account
exists, the anonymous progress will be associated with this user.

Merge anonymous user progress into a Google-authenticated account.

Body: { "session_token": "...", "anonymous_id": "anon_abc123" }

Transfers progress from anonymous_id to the authenticated user:
  - memorize_queue
  - memorize_progress (via queue)
  - quiz_progress
  - hebrew_progress

get.description:
Get current user info from a session token.

Query param: session_token=...
Returns user info or 401 if invalid.

Aggregate all progress data for a user — quiz, memorize, Hebrew, chat.

Returns structured data that can be fed to an LLM for personalized
feedback, study recommendations, and app improvement insights.

Explore the connection graph from a starting verse or TG topic.

Returns nodes and edges for force-directed graph rendering.
Supports `tg:topic_slug` and `bd:entry_slug` as verse parameter.
Search verses, TG topics, and BD entries. Returns autocomplete suggestions.
Get the most connected verses (hub verses) ranked by degree centrality.
Get a human-readable explanation of why two verses or topics are connected.
List Topical Guide topics with metadata.
Get a single Topical Guide topic with its full details and connected verses.
Get a Bible Dictionary entry.
List all hub notes with user progress summary.
Get a full hub note with all steps, verse text, and TG topics.

post.description: Mark a hub note step as complete.

get.description: Return the ordered graph path traversal for a hub note.

post.description:
Grade an open-ended answer using the LLM with a transparent rubric.

Evaluates the QUALITY OF REASONING, not whether the answer is "right" or "wrong."
Returns scores for: engagement with text, internal consistency, 
awareness of alternatives, context.
If user_id is provided, injects their learning progress for personalized feedback.

get.description:
Get provenance info for connections from a verse — shows tradition, hermeneutic, and consensus.

This lets users see WHICH traditions recognize each connection and whether
it's a linguistic fact or an interpretive claim.
Get all tradition labels with their icons and descriptions.

post.description:
Submit an open-ended answer for LLM grading.

Body: {
    "question": "What connections do you see between these passages?",
    "passages": ["gen.1.1", "john.1.1"],
    "user_answer": "Both passages begin with 'In the beginning'...",
    "tier": "analysis"
}
Returns scores + feedback, NOT a right/wrong judgment.

get.description: List all verses in the memorize queue with progress.

post.description: Add a verse to the memorize queue.

delete.description: Remove a verse from the memorize queue.

post.description: Add all verses in a chapter to the memorize queue.

get.description: Get due reviews from the memorize queue, ordered by urgency.

post.description: Submit a rating for a review (1=Again, 2=Hard, 3=Good, 4=Easy).

get.description: List all learning modules with user progress.
Get a full learning module with lesson content, worked examples, and practice questions.

post.description: Submit a practice answer for a module question.

get.description: Get due module reviews (FSRS-scheduled).
Get verse text with connections — served from RAM cache or SQLite.

parameters.name: show_signals

schema.description: Enrich connections with quality signal breakdown
schema.title: Show Signals

parameters.description: Enrich connections with quality signal breakdown

parameters.name: context

schema.description: Number of surrounding verses to include for context window (e.g. context=3 gives ±3 verses)
schema.title: Context

parameters.description: Number of surrounding verses to include for context window (e.g. context=3 gives ±3 verses)

get.description:
Get filtered connections for a verse.

Filters:
  layer: only show this layer
  min_quality: minimum quality tier (verified, strong, probable, suggested)
  discovered_by: only show connections found by this method (text, tsk, llm, algorithm)
  min_confidence: minimum overall confidence (0.0-1.0)
  show_signals: if true, enrich each connection with the full signal breakdown

Search across English, Hebrew, and Greek simultaneously.

Supports query syntax:
  "exact phrase"  — exact phrase match
  -word           — exclude verses containing word
  book:gen        — scope to a specific book ID
  work:ot         — scope to an entire work

English uses FTS5 full-text search (50-100x faster than LIKE).
Hebrew searches against niqqud-stripped words (hebrew_plain).
Look up gematria values. Provide a Hebrew word or a numerical value.
Explore hidden patterns — Atbash, acrostics, advanced gematria.
Instant passage guide from RAM cache — sub-ms, zero disk.

Semantic search — find verses by meaning, not keywords.

Uses sqlite-vec vectors pre-computed by scripts/embed_verses.py.
Returns verses ranked by cosine similarity to the query.
Get connections grouped by PaRDeS interpretation level.
Get database and system statistics.
Search the scripture lexicon by lemma number, Hebrew word, or English translation.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search term — lemma, Hebrew, or English
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Search term — lemma, Hebrew, or English

get.description: Get full lexicon entry for a lemma (Strong's number).
Get all lemmas sharing a triconsonantal root.
Browse all lemmas in a semantic domain.
List all semantic domains.
Get all verses containing a lemma (Strong's number).
Get a verse with morphologically-tagged words — grammar coloring data.

Get word-level grammar/gematria data for all verses in a chapter.

/api/v1/grammar/isa.55  → Word data for all verses in Isaiah 55

Get all non-structural connections for a chapter (intertextual, geographic, etc.).

/api/v1/connections/chapter/isa.55

Get footnotes for a verse or chapter.

/api/v1/footnotes/isa.55.6     → Footnotes for Isa 55:6
/api/v1/footnotes/isa.55       → All footnotes in Isa 55

Get Treasury of Scripture Knowledge cross-references for a verse or chapter.

/api/v1/tsk-crossrefs/isa.55.6     → TSK refs for Isa 55:6
/api/v1/tsk-crossrefs/isa.55       → All TSK refs in Isa 55
Get genealogical connections for a person entity.

Get all Old Testament quotations used in the New Testament.

Aggregates direct_quotation, allusion, prophetic_fulfillment, 
and modified_quotation connections from OT→NT.

post.description:
Submit user feedback on a connection.

Confirming a connection increments its confirmation_count.
Users can confirm, reject, or mark a connection as unclear.
This feedback improves the quality signal over time.

get.description: Get the current status and signals for a specific connection.

Call any registered tool by name — auto-generated from the shared tool registry.

Query params are passed as tool arguments. For complex tools, use POST.
See /docs for schema-per-tool documentation (coming in next iteration).

post.description: Call any registered tool by name with JSON body arguments.

get.description: List all available tools with their schemas.
Get a wiki article about a biblical entity or concept.
Search wiki articles by title or summary.
Browse wiki articles by type (entity, concept, etc.).
Get key verses for an entity from its wiki article.

Get wiki articles related to a knowledge item — 'learn more' links.

Given a knowledge_item_id, finds entities associated with both
the source and target verses, then returns matching wiki articles.

Used by the assessment system to show 'Learn more about [Entity]'
when a user gets a question wrong.

/api/v1/learn/12345  → wiki articles for entities in both verses

Start an assessment filtered to a specific entity's verses.

Returns assessment items where both the source and target verse
mention the given entity. Links from wiki entity pages.

/api/v1/assess/entity/covenant  → assessment on covenant connections

Get Isaiah side-by-side with its Book of Mormon parallel.

Isaiah chapter N maps to:
  2-14 -> 2 Nephi N+10
  48-49 -> 1 Nephi 20-21
  50-53 -> Mosiah/2 Nephi parallels

Get parallelism data for an Isaiah chapter.

Returns verses with their parallelism relationships (synonymous, antithetic,
synthetic, staircase chains) and chiastic structures for the chapter.

Get book-wide chiastic structure overview for Isaiah.

Returns all known chiasms with their section ranges, labels,
and scholar info, suitable for rendering a structure diagram.

Get a full chapter with connections, lines, and intra-verse parallelism.

/api/v1/chapter/isa.55      → All data for Isaiah 55
/api/v1/chapter/matt.5      → All data for Matthew 5
/api/v1/chapter/gen.1       → All data for Genesis 1

Get all entities (people, places, concepts) for verses in a chapter.

/api/v1/chapter/isa.55/entities   → Entities in Isaiah 55
/api/v1/chapter/gen.1/entities    → Entities in Genesis 1
List forum topics, optionally filtered by category.
Get a forum topic with its posts.

post.description: Create a new post in a forum topic.

get.description: Get comments/annotations on a specific verse.

post.description: Add a comment/annotation to a verse.

get.description: Health check with DB integrity, cache status, version, and uptime.
List staging connections (proposed by LLM/UI).
